title: ‘If all else fails, sue’: how ultra-processed food firms are using the courts to obstruct health rules
excerpt: Exclusive: Global investigation reveals 235 lawsuits have been brought against governments in five countries since 2010
content_html:
The world’s biggest ultra-processed food companies have filed scores of lawsuits challenging government policies that tackle junk diets, the Guardian can reveal.

Ultra-processed food (UPF) is linked to harm in every major organ system of the human body and poses a seismic threat to global health. Worldwide consumption is rising and UPF now makes up as much as half the average diet in many countries, including the UK, the US and Australia.

Governments around the world have adopted measures to fight back and safeguard the health of children and adults. Among the regulations are front-of-pack warning labels, marketing restrictions on unhealthy products to make them less alluring and taxes on junk food.

Publicly, the world’s biggest UPF companies insist they support the policies, are committed to helping consumers make informed choices and want to be part of the solution.

But an investigation by the Guardian, in collaboration with academics, the non-profit newsroom Lighthouse Reports and a coalition of media partners across four continents, found many of these same companies are taking governments to court to overturn, weaken or delay the policies.

In total, 235 lawsuits were lodged over health policies targeting UPF in Mexico, Colombia, Brazil, the US and the UK from 2010 to 2025, the investigation found.

It is not clear who is behind every lawsuit. However, three-quarters were filed by UPF manufacturers or trade associations representing them, the investigation found. Some of the corporations involved in the legal cases, which can be revealed for the first time, requested courts withhold their names.

Of the cases filed by companies where the plaintiff was identifiable, 38% were brought by eight parent corporations: Coca-Cola, PepsiCo, Mondelēz, Kellogg’s, Danone, Ferrero, Xignux and Heartland Food Products Group.

Dozens of cases are ongoing. Of those that have been resolved, three-quarters were lost by the food giants. But the length of some of the legal battles added up to almost 600 years in court, the investigation found.

Some of the ultimately unsuccessful lawsuits delayed the adoption of vital public health policies for years, while also tying up government and public health officials in complex and expensive litigation.

In the meantime, the lawsuits are prolonging the growing global public health crisis, and costing countries billions in legal and healthcare costs, experts say.

While governments are being sued over policies aimed at tackling unhealthy diets, the amount of UPF consumed continues to expand, contributing to rising cases of obesity, type 2 diabetes, heart disease, mental ill health and other conditions.

The world’s biggest UPF firms would “not fight so hard” if policies to curb intake of their products were ineffective, said Marion Nestle, a professor of nutrition, food studies and public health at New York University. “The lawsuits tell us that public health measures reduce sales of unhealthful products.”

While the majority of cases were eventually won by governments, experts said the intensive litigation by UPF companies suggested other objectives: slowing down the implementation of laws, limiting the desire and capacity of countries to enact other public health measures and chilling regulators.

The tactics mirrored those used by the tobacco industry for decades, Nestle said, adding: “Food companies are well trained. They follow the tobacco industry playbook to the letter. When all else fails, sue.”

Litigation by tobacco companies is well documented. They have repeatedly sued governments over laws mandating that colourful, branded cigarette packs be replaced with plain ones displaying prominent health warnings, including graphic pictures of rotting teeth, diseased organs and cancerous tumours.

Although the tobacco lawsuits were also mostly unsuccessful, they imposed huge stress, workloads and costs on governments trying to turn the tide on public health.

“The UPF industry appears to be adopting the same strategy,” said Phillip Baker, an associate professor at the University of Sydney who studies food systems and the rising global burden of UPF.

“The UPF industry and its affiliates have persistently challenged front-of-pack warning labels, marketing restrictions and taxation through the courts, concentrated in the years immediately following each policy’s adoption.

“That courts have upheld public health regulation in the large majority of resolved cases is an encouraging signal. But the continued volume of litigation, the years-long duration of some proceedings, and the opacity surrounding a substantial share of corporate plaintiffs together indicate that litigation functions as much as, if not more to delay, deter and constrain as to overturn.”

UPF is made using industrial methods and ingredients not typically found in shops. It often contains additives like flavourings, colourings or emulsifiers to make it appear more attractive and palatable.

Evidence reviewed by 43 of the world’s leading experts and published in The Lancet journal last year suggested that diets high in UPF are linked to overeating, poor nutritional quality and higher exposure to harmful chemicals and additives.

Because they are often cheap, convenient and accessible, they are rapidly displacing fresh food in diets on every continent. In response, countries are increasingly adopting policies designed to curb the harmful effects of UPF.

But these measures have been met with fierce resistance. UPF corporations are using a range of aggressive tactics to drive consumption and prevent regulation. These tactics include lobbying policymakers, launching public-private partnerships and skewering scientific debate.

Last year, the Guardian revealed that the UK government dropped new guidance urging shops to promote minimally processed and nutritious food after a lobbying campaign by UPF companies.

In 2024, the Guardian exposed how three out of five scientists on an expert panel that suggested UPF was being unfairly demonised had ties to the world’s largest manufacturers of the products.

In the first multi-country analysis of its kind, the Guardian and other media outlets worked with researchers from the Robert and Ethel Kennedy Human Rights Centre, the University of Sydney, the University of São Paulo and the University of Caldas to investigate the scale of the UPF industry’s legal pushback.

The investigation identified 235 lawsuits filed over policies targeting UPF in Mexico, Colombia, Brazil, the US and the UK between 2010 and 2025. Labelling on food items was the policy that governments were most sued over, followed by taxes on junk food and marketing restrictions.

That is not surprising, according to Melissa Mialon, an associate professor at Inserm, the French national institute of health and medical research.

She said: “The industry doesn’t want front-of-pack labelling because it’s a first step toward classifying the products for its healthiness or not, and then it gets easier to tax them, it gets easier to put bans on them for marketing and those types of things.”

Most lawsuits were filed in Mexico (193), Colombia (18) and Brazil (17), the investigation found.

“This makes sense as Latin America has been a global leader in adopting some of the first strong food environment policies, making it a key testing ground for both public health innovation and industry resistance,” said Katherine Shats, a legal specialist in child nutrition at Unicef.

Mexico is among several countries that have explicitly recommended avoiding or limiting UPF or “highly processed foods”.

“We saw similar patterns with tobacco control laws, where Australia faced many lawsuits and challenges when it began to introduce plain packaging and other effective tobacco control measures,” said Shats.

There were six lawsuits in the US and one in the UK, the investigation found. Chris van Tulleken, a professor of infection and global health at University College London and the author of Ultra-Processed People, said: “The food industry doesn’t need to bother bringing legal action in the UK very often because our regulations are so shot through with loopholes and exemptions.

“This is partly because for the last three decades industry has helped design the rules that regulate unhealthy food so obviously these regulations aren’t functional.”

The UK policy environment is so deeply embedded with food companies that there is “complete policy paralysis” because of the threat of litigation, van Tulleken said. “People in the government are terrified of these lawsuits.”

“Ultimately, governments need to be bold,” said Shats. “These measures are about safeguarding children, protecting food systems, and addressing a food environment that is driving ill health across entire populations.

“Delaying action due to fear of litigation risks prolonging preventable harm that is much more difficult and costly to address in the longer term.”

A Coca-Cola spokesperson said: “We believe public health challenges are best addressed through collaboration among governments, public health authorities, the private sector and civil society. We engage constructively on topics affecting our business and respect the role each stakeholder plays in advancing public health goals.”

A Ferrero spokesperson said it supported public health objectives aimed at reducing obesity and diet-related disease. They added: “Where Ferrero has engaged with different authorities and regulators, this has concerned clarity around policy implementation – such as inconsistencies in how comparable products are treated under the same rule – rather than opposition to public health objectives.”

A Danone spokesperson said: “We are convinced that businesses should play a positive role, alongside governments, public health institutions and other stakeholders, in addressing public health challenges. We are committed to open dialogue and engaging constructively with policymakers around the world.”

PepsiCo, Mondelēz, Kellogg’s, Xignux and Heartland Food Products Group did not respond to a request for comment.

This investigation was produced in collaboration with Lighthouse Reports, Agência Pública (Brazil), Cuestión Pública (Colombia), Follow the Money (the Netherlands), Il Fatto Alimentare (Italy), L’Espresso (Italy), O Joio e O Trigo (Brazil), Quinto Elemento Lab (Mexico), Santa Cruz Local (US) and The Wire (India)

blocks.html_en: The world’s biggest ultra-processed food companies have filed scores of lawsuits challenging government policies that tackle junk diets, the Guardian can reveal.

sentences.en: The world’s biggest ultra-processed food companies have filed scores of lawsuits challenging government policies that tackle junk diets, the Guardian can reveal.
sentences.zh: 据《卫报》透露，全球最大的超加工食品公司已提起数十起诉讼，挑战政府处理垃圾饮食的政策。

blocks.html_en: Ultra-processed food (UPF) is linked to harm in every major organ system of the human body and poses a seismic threat to global health. Worldwide consumption is rising and UPF now makes up as much as half the average diet in many countries, including the UK, the US and Australia.

sentences.en: Ultra-processed food (UPF) is linked to harm in every major organ system of the human body and poses a seismic threat to global health.
sentences.zh: 超加工食品（UPF）与人体每个主要器官系统的危害有关，并对全球健康构成巨大威胁。

sentences.en: Worldwide consumption is rising and UPF now makes up as much as half the average diet in many countries, including the UK, the US and Australia.
sentences.zh: 全球消费量正在上升，UPF 目前在许多国家（包括英国、美国和澳大利亚）的平均饮食中所占比例高达一半。

blocks.html_en: Governments around the world have adopted measures to fight back and safeguard the health of children and adults. Among the regulations are front-of-pack warning labels, marketing restrictions on unhealthy products to make them less alluring and taxes on junk food.

sentences.en: Governments around the world have adopted measures to fight back and safeguard the health of children and adults.
sentences.zh: 世界各国政府已采取措施反击并保障儿童和成人的健康。

sentences.en: Among the regulations are front-of-pack warning labels, marketing restrictions on unhealthy products to make them less alluring and taxes on junk food.
sentences.zh: 这些法规包括包装正面的警告标签、对不健康产品的营销限制以降低其吸引力以及对垃圾食品征税。

blocks.html_en: Publicly, the world’s biggest UPF companies insist they support the policies, are committed to helping consumers make informed choices and want to be part of the solution.

sentences.en: Publicly, the world’s biggest UPF companies insist they support the policies, are committed to helping consumers make informed choices and want to be part of the solution.
sentences.zh: 在公开场合，全球最大的 UPF 公司坚称他们支持这些政策，致力于帮助消费者做出明智的选择，并希望成为解决方案的一部分。

blocks.html_en: But an investigation by the Guardian, in collaboration with academics, the non-profit newsroom Lighthouse Reports and a coalition of media partners across four continents, found many of these same companies are taking governments to court to overturn, weaken or delay the policies.

sentences.en: But an investigation by the Guardian, in collaboration with academics, the non-profit newsroom Lighthouse Reports and a coalition of media partners across four continents, found many of these same companies are taking governments to court to overturn, weaken or delay the policies.
sentences.zh: 但《卫报》与学术界、非营利新闻机构 Lighthouse Reports 以及四大洲媒体合作伙伴联盟合作进行的一项调查发现，许多此类公司正在将政府告上法庭，以推翻、削弱或推迟政策。

blocks.html_en: In total, 235 lawsuits were lodged over health policies targeting UPF in Mexico, Colombia, Brazil, the US and the UK from 2010 to 2025, the investigation found.

sentences.en: In total, 235 lawsuits were lodged over health policies targeting UPF in Mexico, Colombia, Brazil, the US and the UK from 2010 to 2025, the investigation found.

Some of the corporations involved in the legal cases, which can be revealed for the first time, requested courts withhold their names.

sentences.en: However, three-quarters were filed by UPF manufacturers or trade associations representing them, the investigation found.
sentences.zh: 然而，调查发现，四分之三是由 UPF 制造商或代表他们的行业协会提交的。

sentences.en: Some of the corporations involved in the legal cases, which can be revealed for the first time, requested courts withhold their names.
sentences.zh: 一些首次被披露的涉及法律案件的公司要求法院隐去他们的名字。

blocks.html_en: Of the cases filed by companies where the plaintiff was identifiable, 38% were brought by eight parent corporations: Coca-Cola, PepsiCo, Mondelēz, Kellogg’s, Danone, Ferrero, Xignux and Heartland Food Products Group.

sentences.en: Of the cases filed by companies where the plaintiff was identifiable, 38% were brought by eight parent corporations: Coca-Cola, PepsiCo, Mondelēz, Kellogg’s, Danone, Ferrero, Xignux and Heartland Food Products Group.
sentences.zh: 在可识别原告的公司提起的案件中，38% 是由八家母公司提起的：可口可乐、百事可乐、亿滋、家乐氏、达能、费列罗、Xignux 和 Heartland Food Products Group。

blocks.html_en: Dozens of cases are ongoing. Of those that have been resolved, three-quarters were lost by the food giants. But the length of some of the legal battles added up to almost 600 years in court, the investigation found.

sentences.en: Dozens of cases are ongoing.
sentences.zh: 数十起案件正在审理中。

sentences.en: Of those that have been resolved, three-quarters were lost by the food giants.
sentences.zh: 在已解决的问题中，四分之三都被食品巨头遗失了。

sentences.en: But the length of some of the legal battles added up to almost 600 years in court, the investigation found.
sentences.zh: 但调查发现，一些法庭诉讼的时间加起来长达近 600 年。

blocks.html_en: Some of the ultimately unsuccessful lawsuits delayed the adoption of vital public health policies for years, while also tying up government and public health officials in complex and expensive litigation.

sentences.en: Some of the ultimately unsuccessful lawsuits delayed the adoption of vital public health policies for years, while also tying up government and public health officials in complex and expensive litigation.
sentences.zh: 一些最终不成功的诉讼将重要公共卫生政策的采纳推迟了数年，同时也让政府和公共卫生官员陷入复杂而昂贵的诉讼中。

blocks.html_en: In the meantime, the lawsuits are prolonging the growing global public health crisis, and costing countries billions in legal and healthcare costs, experts say.

sentences.en: In the meantime, the lawsuits are prolonging the growing global public health crisis, and costing countries billions in legal and healthcare costs, experts say.
sentences.zh: 与此同时，专家表示，这些诉讼正在延长日益严重的全球公共卫生危机，并给各国带来数十亿美元的法律和医疗费用。

blocks.html_en: While governments are being sued over policies aimed at tackling unhealthy diets, the amount of UPF consumed continues to expand, contributing to rising cases of obesity, type 2 diabetes, heart disease, mental ill health and other conditions.

sentences.en: While governments are being sued over policies aimed at tackling unhealthy diets, the amount of UPF consumed continues to expand, contributing to rising cases of obesity, type 2 diabetes, heart disease, mental ill health and other conditions.
sentences.zh: 尽管各国政府因旨在解决不健康饮食问题的政策而受到起诉，但 UPF 的消费量仍在不断增加，导致肥胖、2 型糖尿病、心脏病、精神疾病和其他疾病的病例不断增加。

sentences.en: Marion Nestle, a professor of nutrition at New York University, accuses food manufacturers of using the tactics deployed by tobacco companies.

sentences.zh: “诉讼告诉我们，公共卫生措施减少了不健康产品的销售。”

blocks.html_en: The tactics mirrored those used by the tobacco industry for decades, Nestle said, adding: “Food companies are well trained. They follow the tobacco industry playbook to the letter. When all else fails, sue.”

sentences.en: They follow the tobacco industry playbook to the letter.
sentences.zh: 他们严格遵循烟草业的剧本。

sentences.en: When all else fails, sue.”
sentences.zh: 当一切都失败时，就起诉。”

blocks.html_en: “The UPF industry appears to be adopting the same strategy,” said Phillip Baker, an associate professor at the University of Sydney who studies food systems and the rising global burden of UPF.

sentences.en: “The UPF industry appears to be adopting the same strategy,” said Phillip Baker, an associate professor at the University of Sydney who studies food systems and the rising global burden of UPF.
sentences.zh: “UPF 行业似乎正在采取同样的策略，”悉尼大学副教授 Phillip Baker 说，他研究食品系统和全球日益增加的 UPF 负担。

blocks.html_en: “The UPF industry and its affiliates have persistently challenged front-of-pack warning labels, marketing restrictions and taxation through the courts, concentrated in the years immediately following each policy’s adoption.

sentences.en: “The UPF industry and its affiliates have persistently challenged front-of-pack warning labels, marketing restrictions and taxation through the courts, concentrated in the years immediately following each policy’s adoption.
sentences.zh: “UPF 行业及其附属机构一直通过法院对包装正面的警告标签、营销限制和税收提出质疑，这些挑战集中在每项政策通过后的几年里。

blocks.html_en: “That courts have upheld public health regulation in the large majority of resolved cases is an encouraging signal. But the continued volume of litigation, the years-long duration of some proceedings, and the opacity surrounding a substantial share of corporate plaintiffs together indicate that litigation functions as much as, if not more to delay, deter and constrain as to overturn.”

sentences.en: “That courts have upheld public health regulation in the large majority of resolved cases is an encouraging signal.
sentences.zh: “法院在绝大多数已解决的案件中都支持公共卫生监管，这是一个令人鼓舞的信号。

sentences.en: But the continued volume of litigation, the years-long duration of some proceedings, and the opacity surrounding a substantial share of corporate plaintiffs together indicate that litigation functions as much as, if not more to delay, deter and constrain as to overturn.”
sentences.zh: 但持续不断的诉讼数量、一些诉讼程序长达数年的持续时间以及相当大一部分公司原告的不透明性共同表明，诉讼的作用与拖延、威慑和限制推翻的作用一样大，甚至更多。”

blocks.html_en: UPF is made using industrial methods and ingredients not typically found in shops. It often contains additives like flavourings, colourings or emulsifiers to make it appear more attractive and palatable.

sentences.en: UPF is made using industrial methods and ingredients not typically found in shops.
sentences.zh: UPF 采用工业方法和商店中常见的成分制成。

sentences.en: It often contains additives like flavourings, colourings or emulsifiers to make it appear more attractive and palatable.
sentences.zh: 它通常含有调味剂、色素或乳化剂等添加剂，以使其看起来更具吸引力和可口性。

blocks.html_en: Evidence reviewed by 43 of the world’s leading experts and published in The Lancet journal last year suggested that diets high in UPF are linked to overeating, poor nutritional quality and higher exposure to harmful chemicals and additives.

sentences.en: Evidence reviewed by 43 of the world’s leading experts and published in The Lancet journal last year suggested that diets high in UPF are linked to overeating, poor nutritional quality and higher exposure to harmful chemicals and additives.
sentences.zh: 去年发表在《柳叶刀》杂志上的证据由 43 位世界顶尖专家审查，表明 UPF 含量高的饮食与暴饮暴食、营养质量差以及接触有害化学物质和添加剂较多有关。

blocks.html_en: Because they are often cheap, convenient and accessible, they are rapidly displacing fresh food in diets on every continent. In response, countries are increasingly adopting policies designed to curb the harmful effects of UPF.

sentences.en: Because they are often cheap, convenient and accessible, they are rapidly displacing fresh food in diets on every continent.
sentences.zh: 由于它们通常便宜、方便且容易获得，因此它们正在迅速取代各大洲饮食中的新鲜食品。

sentences.en: In response, countries are increasingly adopting policies designed to curb the harmful effects of UPF.
sentences.zh: 为此，各国越来越多地采取旨在遏制 UPF 有害影响的政策。

blocks.html_en: But these measures have been met with fierce resistance. UPF corporations are using a range of aggressive tactics to drive consumption and prevent regulation. These tactics include lobbying policymakers, launching public-private partnerships and skewering scientific debate.

sentences.en: But these measures have been met with fierce resistance.
sentences.zh: 但这些措施遭到了强烈抵制。

sentences.en: UPF corporations are using a range of aggressive tactics to drive consumption and prevent regulation.
sentences.zh: UPF 公司正在使用一系列激进策略来推动消费并阻止监管。

sentences.en: These tactics include lobbying policymakers, launching public-private partnerships and skewering scientific debate.
sentences.zh: 这些策略包括游说政策制定者、启动公私合作伙伴关系以及歪曲科学辩论。

blocks.html_en: Last year, the Guardian revealed that the UK government dropped new guidance urging shops to promote minimally processed and nutritious food after a lobbying campaign by UPF companies.

sentences.en: Last year, the Guardian revealed that the UK government dropped new guidance urging shops to promote minimally processed and nutritious food after a lobbying campaign by UPF companies.
sentences.zh: 去年，《卫报》透露，在 UPF 公司进行游说活动后，英国政府放弃了敦促商店推销经过最低限度加工的营养食品的新指导方针。

blocks.html_en: In 2024, the Guardian exposed how three out of five scientists on an expert panel that suggested UPF was being unfairly demonised had ties to the world’s largest manufacturers of the products.

sentences.en: In 2024, the Guardian exposed how three out of five scientists on an expert panel that suggested UPF was being unfairly demonised had ties to the world’s largest manufacturers of the products.
sentences.zh: 2024 年，《卫报》揭露了专家小组中五分之三的科学家与世界上最大的产品制造商有联系，该专家小组认为 UPF 被不公平地妖魔化。

blocks.html_en: In the first multi-country analysis of its kind, the Guardian and other media outlets worked with researchers from the Robert and Ethel Kennedy Human Rights Centre, the University of Sydney, the University of São Paulo and the University of Caldas to investigate the scale of the UPF industry’s legal pushback.

sentences.en: In the first multi-country analysis of its kind, the Guardian and other media outlets worked with researchers from the Robert and Ethel Kennedy Human Rights Centre, the University of Sydney, the University of São Paulo and the University of Caldas to investigate the scale of the UPF industry’s legal pushback.
sentences.zh: 在此类首次多国分析中，《卫报》和其他媒体机构与罗伯特和埃塞尔·肯尼迪人权中心、悉尼大学、圣保罗大学和卡尔达斯大学的研究人员合作，调查了 UPF 行业法律阻力的规模。

blocks.html_en: The investigation identified 235 lawsuits filed over policies targeting UPF in Mexico, Colombia, Brazil, the US and the UK between 2010 and 2025. Labelling on food items was the policy that governments were most sued over, followed by taxes on junk food and marketing restrictions.

sentences.en: The investigation identified 235 lawsuits filed over policies targeting UPF in Mexico, Colombia, Brazil, the US and the UK between 2010 and 2025.
sentences.zh: 调查发现，2010 年至 2025 年间，墨西哥、哥伦比亚、巴西、美国和英国针对 UPF 政策提起了 235 起诉讼。

sentences.en: Labelling on food items was the policy that governments were most sued over, followed by taxes on junk food and marketing restrictions.
sentences.zh: 食品标签是政府被起诉最多的政策，其次是对垃圾食品征税和营销限制。

blocks.html_en: That is not surprising, according to Melissa Mialon, an associate professor at Inserm, the French national institute of health and medical research.

sentences.en: That is not surprising, according to Melissa Mialon, an associate professor at Inserm, the French national institute of health and medical research.
sentences.zh: 法国国家健康与医学研究所 Inserm 副教授 Melissa Mialon 表示，这并不奇怪。

blocks.html_en: She said: “The industry doesn’t want front-of-pack labelling because it’s a first step toward classifying the products for its healthiness or not, and then it gets easier to tax them, it gets easier to put bans on them for marketing and those types of things.”

sentences.en: She said: “The industry doesn’t want front-of-pack labelling because it’s a first step toward classifying the products for its healthiness or not, and then it gets easier to tax them, it gets easier to put bans on them for marketing and those types of things.”
sentences.zh: 她说：“该行业不希望在包装正面贴上标签，因为这是对产品健康与否进行分类的第一步，然后对它们征税就会变得更容易，对它们进行营销和此类行为的禁令也变得更容易。”

blocks.html_en: Most lawsuits were filed in Mexico (193), Colombia (18) and Brazil (17), the investigation found.

sentences.en: Most lawsuits were filed in Mexico (193), Colombia (18) and Brazil (17), the investigation found.
sentences.zh: 调查发现，大多数诉讼是在墨西哥（193 起）、哥伦比亚（18 起）和巴西（17 起）提起的。

blocks.html_en: “This makes sense as Latin America has been a global leader in adopting some of the first strong food environment policies, making it a key testing ground for both public health innovation and industry resistance,” said Katherine Shats, a legal specialist in child nutrition at Unicef.

sentences.en: “This makes sense as Latin America has been a global leader in adopting some of the first strong food environment policies, making it a key testing ground for both public health innovation and industry resistance,” said Katherine Shats, a legal specialist in child nutrition at Unicef.
sentences.zh: 联合国儿童基金会儿童营养方面的法律专家凯瑟琳·沙茨 (Katherine Shats) 表示：“这是有道理的，因为拉丁美洲在率先采取一些强有力的食品环境政策方面一直处于全球领先地位，使其成为公共卫生创新和行业阻力的关键试验场。”

blocks.html_en: Mexico is among several countries that have explicitly recommended avoiding or limiting UPF or “highly processed foods”.

sentences.en: Mexico is among several countries that have explicitly recommended avoiding or limiting UPF or “highly processed foods”.
sentences.zh: 墨西哥是明确建议避免或限制 UPF 或“高度加工食品”的几个国家之一。

blocks.html_en: “We saw similar patterns with tobacco control laws, where Australia faced many lawsuits and challenges when it began to introduce plain packaging and other effective tobacco control measures,” said Shats.

sentences.en: “We saw similar patterns with tobacco control laws, where Australia faced many lawsuits and challenges when it began to introduce plain packaging and other effective tobacco control measures,” said Shats.
sentences.zh: “我们在烟草控制法中看到了类似的模式，澳大利亚在开始引入无装饰包装和其他有效的烟草控制措施时面临着许多诉讼和挑战，”沙茨说。

sentences.en: Chris van Tulleken, author of Ultra-Processed People, says government officials are ‘terrified’ of lawsuits from food companies.
sentences.zh: 《超加工人员》一书的作者克里斯·范·图勒肯 (Chris van Tulleken) 表示，政府官员“害怕”食品公司提起诉讼。

sentences.en: Photograph: TT News Agency/Alamy
sentences.zh: 图片来源：TT 通讯社/Alamy

blocks.html_en: There were six lawsuits in the US and one in the UK, the investigation found. Chris van Tulleken, a professor of infection and global health at University College London and the author of Ultra-Processed People, said: “The food industry doesn’t need to bother bringing legal action in the UK very often because our regulations are so shot through with loopholes and exemptions.

sentences.en: There were six lawsuits in the US and one in the UK, the investigation found.
sentences.zh: 调查发现，美国有六起诉讼，英国有一起诉讼。

sentences.en: Chris van Tulleken, a professor of infection and global health at University College London and the author of Ultra-Processed People, said: “The food industry doesn’t need to bother bringing legal action in the UK very often because our regulations are so shot through with loopholes and exemptions.
sentences.zh: 伦敦大学学院感染与全球健康教授、《超加工人员》一书的作者克里斯·范图勒肯 (Chris van Tulleken) 表示：“食品行业不需要经常在英国提起法律诉讼，因为我们的法规充满了漏洞和豁免。

blocks.html_en: “This is partly because for the last three decades industry has helped design the rules that regulate unhealthy food so obviously these regulations aren’t functional.”

sentences.en: “This is partly because for the last three decades industry has helped design the rules that regulate unhealthy food so obviously these regulations aren’t functional.”
sentences.zh: “部分原因是在过去三十年中，行业帮助设计了监管不健康食品的规则，因此显然这些法规不起作用。”

blocks.html_en: The UK policy environment is so deeply embedded with food companies that there is “complete policy paralysis” because of the threat of litigation, van Tulleken said. “People in the government are terrified of these lawsuits.”

sentences.en: The UK policy environment is so deeply embedded with food companies that there is “complete policy paralysis” because of the threat of litigation, van Tulleken said.
sentences.zh: 范图勒肯说，英国的政策环境与食品公司的关系如此之深，以至于由于诉讼的威胁而出现“彻底的政策瘫痪”。

sentences.en: “People in the government are terrified of these lawsuits.”
sentences.zh: “政府人员对这些诉讼感到恐惧。”

blocks.html_en: “Ultimately, governments need to be bold,” said Shats. “These measures are about safeguarding children, protecting food systems, and addressing a food environment that is driving ill health across entire populations.

sentences.en: “Ultimately, governments need to be bold,” said Shats.
sentences.zh: “最终，政府需要大胆，”沙茨说。

sentences.en: “These measures are about safeguarding children, protecting food systems, and addressing a food environment that is driving ill health across entire populations.
sentences.zh: “这些措施旨在保护儿童、保护粮食系统以及解决导致全体人口健康不良的食品环境问题。

blocks.html_en: “Delaying action due to fear of litigation risks prolonging preventable harm that is much more difficult and costly to address in the longer term.”

sentences.en: “Delaying action due to fear of litigation risks prolonging preventable harm that is much more difficult and costly to address in the longer term.”
sentences.zh: “由于担心诉讼而推迟采取行动，可能会延长可预防的损害，从长远来看，解决这种损害更加困难且成本高昂。”

blocks.html_en: A Coca-Cola spokesperson said: “We believe public health challenges are best addressed through collaboration among governments, public health authorities, the private sector and civil society. We engage constructively on topics affecting our business and respect the role each stakeholder plays in advancing public health goals.”

sentences.en: A Coca-Cola spokesperson said: “We believe public health challenges are best addressed through collaboration among governments, public health authorities, the private sector and civil society.
sentences.zh: 可口可乐发言人表示：“我们相信，通过政府、公共卫生当局、私营部门和民间社会之间的合作，可以最好地解决公共卫生挑战。

sentences.en: We engage constructively on topics affecting our business and respect the role each stakeholder plays in advancing public health goals.”
sentences.zh: 我们建设性地参与影响我们业务的话题，并尊重每个利益相关者在推进公共卫生目标方面所发挥的作用。”

blocks.html_en: A Ferrero spokesperson said it supported public health objectives aimed at reducing obesity and diet-related disease. They added: “Where Ferrero has engaged with different authorities and regulators, this has concerned clarity around policy implementation – such as inconsistencies in how comparable products are treated under the same rule – rather than opposition to public health objectives.”

sentences.en: A Ferrero spokesperson said it supported public health objectives aimed at reducing obesity and diet-related disease.
sentences.zh: 费列罗发言人表示，该公司支持旨在减少肥胖和饮食相关疾病的公共卫生目标。

sentences.en: They added: “Where Ferrero has engaged with different authorities and regulators, this has concerned clarity around policy implementation – such as inconsistencies in how comparable products are treated under the same rule – rather than opposition to public health objectives.”
sentences.zh: 他们补充道：“费列罗与不同当局和监管机构合作时，关注的是政策实施的清晰度，例如在同一规则下对待同类产品的不一致，而不是反对公共卫生目标。”

blocks.html_en: A Danone spokesperson said: “We are convinced that businesses should play a positive role, alongside governments, public health institutions and other stakeholders, in addressing public health challenges. We are committed to open dialogue and engaging constructively with policymakers around the world.”

sentences.en: A Danone spokesperson said: “We are convinced that businesses should play a positive role, alongside governments, public health institutions and other stakeholders, in addressing public health challenges.
sentences.zh: 达能发言人表示：“我们坚信，企业应该与政府、公共卫生机构和其他利益相关者一起在应对公共卫生挑战方面发挥积极作用。

sentences.en: We are committed to open dialogue and engaging constructively with policymakers around the world.”
sentences.zh: 我们致力于与世界各地的政策制定者进行公开对话和建设性接触。”

blocks.html_en: PepsiCo, Mondelēz, Kellogg’s, Xignux and Heartland Food Products Group did not respond to a request for comment.

sentences.en: PepsiCo, Mondelēz, Kellogg’s, Xignux and Heartland Food Products Group did not respond to a request for comment.
sentences.zh: 百事可乐、亿滋、家乐氏、Xignux 和 Heartland Food Products Group 均未回应置评请求。

blocks.html_en: This investigation was produced in collaboration with Lighthouse Reports, Agência Pública (Brazil), Cuestión Pública (Colombia), Follow the Money (the Netherlands), Il Fatto Alimentare (Italy), L’Espresso (Italy), O Joio e O Trigo (Brazil), Quinto Elemento Lab (Mexico), Santa Cruz Local (US) and The Wire (India)

sentences.en: This investigation was produced in collaboration with Lighthouse Reports, Agência Pública (Brazil), Cuestión Pública (Colombia), Follow the Money (the Netherlands), Il Fatto Alimentare (Italy), L’Espresso (Italy), O Joio e O Trigo (Brazil), Quinto Elemento Lab (Mexico), Santa Cruz Local (US) and The Wire (India)
sentences.zh: 这项调查是与 Lighthouse Reports、Agência Pública（巴西）、Cuestión Pública（哥伦比亚）、Follow the Money（荷兰）、Il Fatto Alimentare（意大利）、L’Espresso（意大利）、O Joio e O Trigo（巴西）、Quinto Elemento Lab（墨西哥）、Santa Cruz Local（美国）和 The Wire（印度）合作进行的

content_html_zh:
据《卫报》透露，全球最大的超加工食品公司已提起数十起诉讼，挑战政府处理垃圾饮食的政策。

超加工食品（UPF）与人体每个主要器官系统的危害有关，并对全球健康构成巨大威胁。 全球消费量正在上升，UPF 目前在许多国家（包括英国、美国和澳大利亚）的平均饮食中所占比例高达一半。

世界各国政府已采取措施反击并保障儿童和成人的健康。 这些法规包括包装正面的警告标签、对不健康产品的营销限制以降低其吸引力以及对垃圾食品征税。

在公开场合，全球最大的 UPF 公司坚称他们支持这些政策，致力于帮助消费者做出明智的选择，并希望成为解决方案的一部分。

但《卫报》与学术界、非营利新闻机构 Lighthouse Reports 以及四大洲媒体合作伙伴联盟合作进行的一项调查发现，许多此类公司正在将政府告上法庭，以推翻、削弱或推迟政策。

调查发现，2010 年至 2025 年间，墨西哥、哥伦比亚、巴西、美国和英国总共就针对 UPF 的卫生政策提起了 235 起诉讼。

目前尚不清楚每起诉讼的幕后黑手是谁。 然而，调查发现，四分之三是由 UPF 制造商或代表他们的行业协会提交的。 一些首次被披露的涉及法律案件的公司要求法院隐去他们的名字。

在可识别原告的公司提起的案件中，38% 是由八家母公司提起的：可口可乐、百事可乐、亿滋、家乐氏、达能、费列罗、Xignux 和 Heartland Food Products Group。

数十起案件正在审理中。 在已解决的问题中，四分之三都被食品巨头遗失了。 但调查发现，一些法庭诉讼的时间加起来长达近 600 年。

一些最终不成功的诉讼将重要公共卫生政策的采纳推迟了数年，同时也让政府和公共卫生官员陷入复杂而昂贵的诉讼中。

与此同时，专家表示，这些诉讼正在延长日益严重的全球公共卫生危机，并给各国带来数十亿美元的法律和医疗费用。

尽管各国政府因旨在解决不健康饮食问题的政策而受到起诉，但 UPF 的消费量仍在不断增加，导致肥胖、2 型糖尿病、心脏病、精神疾病和其他疾病的病例不断增加。

纽约大学营养、食品研究和公共卫生教授马里昂·内斯特尔表示，如果限制其产品摄入的政策无效，世界上最大的 UPF 公司将“不会那么努力地战斗”。 “诉讼告诉我们，公共卫生措施减少了不健康产品的销售。”

虽然大多数案件最终由政府胜诉，但专家表示，UPF 公司的密集诉讼暗示了其他目标：减缓法律的实施，限制各国制定其他公共卫生措施的愿望和能力，并使监管机构感到畏惧。

雀巢表示，这些策略反映了烟草业几十年来所使用的策略，并补充道：“食品公司训练有素。 他们严格遵循烟草业的剧本。 当一切都失败时，就起诉。”

烟草公司的诉讼有据可查。 他们多次起诉政府，要求政府将彩色品牌卷烟包装更换为带有明显健康警告的普通卷烟包装，其中包括腐烂的牙齿、患病器官和癌性肿瘤的图片。

尽管烟草诉讼也大多不成功，但它们给试图扭转公共卫生潮流的政府带来了巨大的压力、工作量和成本。

“UPF 行业似乎正在采取同样的策略，”悉尼大学副教授 Phillip Baker 说，他研究食品系统和全球日益增加的 UPF 负担。

“UPF 行业及其附属机构一直通过法院对包装正面的警告标签、营销限制和税收提出质疑，这些挑战集中在每项政策通过后的几年里。

“法院在绝大多数已解决的案件中都支持公共卫生监管，这是一个令人鼓舞的信号。 但持续不断的诉讼数量、一些诉讼程序长达数年的持续时间以及相当大一部分公司原告的不透明性共同表明，诉讼的作用与拖延、威慑和限制推翻的作用一样大，甚至更多。”

UPF 采用工业方法和商店中常见的成分制成。 它通常含有调味剂、色素或乳化剂等添加剂，以使其看起来更具吸引力和可口性。

去年发表在《柳叶刀》杂志上的证据由 43 位世界顶尖专家审查，表明 UPF 含量高的饮食与暴饮暴食、营养质量差以及接触有害化学物质和添加剂较多有关。

由于它们通常便宜、方便且容易获得，因此它们正在迅速取代各大洲饮食中的新鲜食品。 为此，各国越来越多地采取旨在遏制 UPF 有害影响的政策。

但这些措施遭到了强烈抵制。 UPF 公司正在使用一系列激进策略来推动消费并阻止监管。 这些策略包括游说政策制定者、启动公私合作伙伴关系以及歪曲科学辩论。

去年，《卫报》透露，在 UPF 公司进行游说活动后，英国政府放弃了敦促商店推销经过最低限度加工的营养食品的新指导方针。

2024 年，《卫报》揭露了专家小组中五分之三的科学家与世界上最大的产品制造商有联系，该专家小组认为 UPF 被不公平地妖魔化。

在此类首次多国分析中，《卫报》和其他媒体机构与罗伯特和埃塞尔·肯尼迪人权中心、悉尼大学、圣保罗大学和卡尔达斯大学的研究人员合作，调查了 UPF 行业法律阻力的规模。

调查发现，2010 年至 2025 年间，墨西哥、哥伦比亚、巴西、美国和英国针对 UPF 政策提起了 235 起诉讼。 食品标签是政府被起诉最多的政策，其次是对垃圾食品征税和营销限制。

法国国家健康与医学研究所 Inserm 副教授 Melissa Mialon 表示，这并不奇怪。

她说：“该行业不希望在包装正面贴上标签，因为这是对产品健康与否进行分类的第一步，然后对它们征税就会变得更容易，对它们进行营销和此类行为的禁令也变得更容易。”

调查发现，大多数诉讼是在墨西哥（193 起）、哥伦比亚（18 起）和巴西（17 起）提起的。

联合国儿童基金会儿童营养方面的法律专家凯瑟琳·沙茨 (Katherine Shats) 表示：“这是有道理的，因为拉丁美洲在率先采取一些强有力的食品环境政策方面一直处于全球领先地位，使其成为公共卫生创新和行业阻力的关键试验场。”

墨西哥是明确建议避免或限制 UPF 或“高度加工食品”的几个国家之一。

“我们在烟草控制法中看到了类似的模式，澳大利亚在开始引入无装饰包装和其他有效的烟草控制措施时面临着许多诉讼和挑战，”沙茨说。

调查发现，美国有六起诉讼，英国有一起诉讼。 伦敦大学学院感染与全球健康教授、《超加工人员》一书的作者克里斯·范图勒肯 (Chris van Tulleken) 表示：“食品行业不需要经常在英国提起法律诉讼，因为我们的法规充满了漏洞和豁免。

“部分原因是在过去三十年中，行业帮助设计了监管不健康食品的规则，因此显然这些法规不起作用。”

范图勒肯说，英国的政策环境与食品公司的关系如此之深，以至于由于诉讼的威胁而出现“彻底的政策瘫痪”。 “政府人员对这些诉讼感到恐惧。”

“最终，政府需要大胆，”沙茨说。 “这些措施旨在保护儿童、保护粮食系统以及解决导致全体人口健康不良的食品环境问题。

“由于担心诉讼而推迟采取行动，可能会延长可预防的损害，从长远来看，解决这种损害更加困难且成本高昂。”

可口可乐发言人表示：“我们相信，通过政府、公共卫生当局、私营部门和民间社会之间的合作，可以最好地解决公共卫生挑战。 我们建设性地参与影响我们业务的话题，并尊重每个利益相关者在推进公共卫生目标方面所发挥的作用。”

费列罗发言人表示，该公司支持旨在减少肥胖和饮食相关疾病的公共卫生目标。 他们补充道：“费列罗与不同当局和监管机构合作时，关注的是政策实施的清晰度，例如在同一规则下对待同类产品的不一致，而不是反对公共卫生目标。”

达能发言人表示：“我们坚信，企业应该与政府、公共卫生机构和其他利益相关者一起在应对公共卫生挑战方面发挥积极作用。 我们致力于与世界各地的政策制定者进行公开对话和建设性接触。”

百事可乐、亿滋、家乐氏、Xignux 和 Heartland Food Products Group 均未回应置评请求。

这项调查是与 Lighthouse Reports、Agência Pública（巴西）、Cuestión Pública（哥伦比亚）、Follow the Money（荷兰）、Il Fatto Alimentare（意大利）、L’Espresso（意大利）、O Joio e O Trigo（巴西）、Quinto Elemento Lab（墨西哥）、Santa Cruz Local（美国）和 The Wire（印度）合作进行的
